nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving or still freshwater habitats — streams, rivers, and shallow pools in tropical Southeast Asia, where it grows fully submerged, a habit uncommon among sedges of the Cyperaceae family, most of which grow only above the waterline.

decorPreferences: nutrient-rich aquasoil or fine-grained planted-tank substrate
open background planting space allowing long leaves to sway

goodWith: nature-style and Southeast Asian biotope aquascapes
avoidWith: large fish that uproot rosette plants

summary: Cyperus Helferi is a Thai sedge with long, grass-like leaves that sway gracefully in moving water. One of the few members of the Cyperaceae family that grows reliably submerged, it is generally kept as a background plant and grows best with nutrient-rich substrate and medium-to-high light.
careNotes:
Cyperus Helferi is a Southeast Asian sedge (family Cyperaceae) native to Thailand and Myanmar, and is one of the few members of its family that grows reliably underwater rather than only above the waterline. It forms a distinctive grass-like rosette of long, narrow, ribbon-like leaves that tend to sway gracefully in moving water, and is typically used as a background plant where it commonly reaches 20–50 cm in height with a rosette diameter of around 15–25 cm.

Lighting needs are generally described as medium to high, and CO2 supplementation is broadly recommended to encourage steady growth — without it, growth is even slower than the plant's already-slow baseline. Water temperature in the 22–28 °C range is generally suitable, with a slight preference for the cooler end of that band. A pH of 6.0 to 7.5 is tolerated, with a tendency toward soft, slightly acidic water; general hardness (gH) anywhere from 1 to 15 °dH is commonly acceptable.

Cyperus Helferi is a heavy root feeder and tends to do best in a fine-grained, nutrient-rich planted-tank substrate — aquasoil or a similar planted-tank substrate — supplemented with root tabs. Comprehensive water-column fertilization covering nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, and micronutrients including iron is also generally helpful. Despite being a heavy root feeder, its root structure is relatively delicate, so gentle to moderate water flow is preferable and large, plant-uprooting fish should be avoided. Plant single rosettes into the substrate carefully; trimming damaged outer leaves is commonly advised to encourage fresh growth from the centre.
breedingNotes: Propagation is vegetative: mature plants develop adventitious daughter plantlets at the base of the mother rosette, which can be separated once they have their own root systems and replanted. Tip cuttings replanted into nutrient-rich substrate are also documented but less common than dividing daughter plants.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Plant details page resolved from Tropica search for Cyperus helferi. Provided height (20–30+ cm), rosette diameter (15–25 cm), medium light demand (~0.5 W/L), medium CO2 (6–14 mg/L), slow growth rate, Asian/Thai origin, and medium difficulty.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked family (Cyperaceae), height (8–20+ in / ~20–50 cm), pH 6.0–7.5, recommended temperature below ~27 °C / 80 °F, medium-to-high light, slow growth, easy care rating, background placement, and propagation by cutting new growth with attached roots.

additional.name: The Aquarium Guide
additional.notes: Used for temperature range (20–30 °C, ideal 25–27 °C), pH 5–7.5, hardness 1–20 °dH, lighting (2–4 W/gal), CO2 recommendation, nutrient-rich substrate and NPK+micronutrient fertilization guidance, ~100 L (≈ 20 gal) minimum tank size, adventitious daughter-plant propagation, and a more cautious 'intermediate-to-advanced' care assessment. Care level averaged across the three sources to 'intermediate'; pH range centered to 6.0–7.5; gH centered to 1–15.
